verdict: CRITERION MET, but the fix is WINDOW SIZE, not multiscale: fragile short window 2/15 -> multiscale 15/15; however a single LARGE window already reaches 15/15 (median err 0), matching multiscale. In this synthetic setup the 5/15 failure is a window-size artifact (short windows are fooled by transient excursions); enlarging the analysis window fixes it, and the multiscale bank adds no measured value over a single large window. Drift guardrail holds (prominence seam 16.3 > drift 5.4). Caveats: symmetric windows (offline localization, not strictly causal/online); real EEG may impose a precision cost on a large window that a multiscale bank could escape -- untested without PhysioNet.
